title: Proclamation No. 389, s. 1965
text:
MALACAÑANG RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 389

DECLARING APRIL 30, 1965, AS WOMEN’S RIGHTS DAY.

In order that our people nay know and appreciate the important role played by women in the economic, social and political progress of the nation, I, DIOSDADO MACAPAGAL, President of the Philippines, by virtue of the powers vested in me by law, do hereby declare April 30, 1965, as Women’s Rights Day.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 12th day of April, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-five.

(SGD.) DIOSDADO MACAPAGAL President of the Philippines

By the President:

(SGD.) RAMON A. DIAZ Executive Secretary

Source: Presidential Museum and Library
